item.title: Gmail Legal-Notice Phishing Uses short.gy to Hide Credential-Harvest Domain
item.content:encoded:
Legal Threat in Your Inbox: How a Gmail Shortlink Hides a Credential-Harvest Destination — Should Read

A phishing email posing as a bilingual legal notice used a legitimate Gmail account and a short.gy link to hide a malicious credential-harvesting domain, allowing SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and basic URL reputation checks to appear clean. The key lesson is that defenders must analyze sender context and follow shortened URLs through the full redirect chain to detect these attacks.

  • An anonymized sender using a personal Gmail account sent a mass-BCC email with a bilingual Hebrew and English subject line that framed the message as a legal notice.
  • The email body contained a short.gy shortener link, uepk9u.short.gy/DJp0Jm, which resolved to hxxps://feature.chongdaotang.net/Wx6Ld3KlNmuU4tT@tDVpJltq5/.
  • IRONSCALES link analysis rated the resolved destination on chongdaotang.net as Malicious, while the short.gy shortener domain itself carried clean reputation with major URL scanning services.
  • SPF, DKIM, and DMARC all passed because the message genuinely originated from Google infrastructure through a real Gmail account.
  • IRONSCALES Adaptive AI flagged the message at 90% confidence based on signals including a first-contact sender, a URL-shortener-wrapped attacker domain, high-pressure legal-threat framing, and BCC delivery.

item.title: Russian SMS Phishing Targets Messaging Accounts; Clean GitHub Repos Abuse Claude Code
OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol Preview and Healthcare Invoice Phishing via SaaS Redirects
